violations.text: Bottle of cooking wine observed in the hand-washing sink. Chef observed filling a pan from hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2025

Foods in the tabletop cold hold unit observed at 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Ice placed under the food containers for lunch service and service call placed. No TCS foods to be stored in this unit until it is maintaining a consistent temperature at/below 41 degrees F. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the bar dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of black residue and other debris. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2024
Strainer observed in the hand-washing sink at the bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of pink and black residues. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine is being cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2024

Food products in the tabletop cold hold unit observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Products have been in this unit for 2 hours. Ice was placed under the bins for dinner service and service call placed. TCS foods are not to be stored in this unit until it can maintain consistent temperatures at/below 41 degrees F. New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2024
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2025

Shrimp was observed thawing in the sink. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2024
